action: Patriots fortify east bank under Caswell and Lillington; bridge planks removed and grease applied to the bare log girders; two artillery pieces — 'Old Mother Covington and her daughter' — positioned to cover the crossing
Loyalist column encamped approximately 6 miles west; Brig. Gen. MacDonald ill and unable to command; Lt. Col. MacLeod in effective command; pre-dawn march ordered for February 27
Patriot force holds east bank in the dark; artillery positioned; men ordered to silence and hold fire until the signal
Column arrives west bank before dawn; scouts discover the Patriot west-bank outpost abandoned; MacLeod misreads the position as flight; orders advance across the bridge
Patriots hold fire; artillery and muskets ready at the breastwork; pipers heard on the far bank
MacLeod and Campbell lead approximately 80 Highland swordsmen with broadswords drawn across the bare, greased log girders of the dismantled bridge; pipers playing; main column held on west bank
Patriots open fire at approximately 30 paces with cannon and musketry; MacLeod and Campbell fall at the bridge; Moore reports MacLeod died 'with upward of twenty balls through him'
Advance company annihilated at the bridge; MacLeod and Campbell KIA; surviving swordsmen fall or retreat; main column on west bank recoils from the fire

Patriot pursuit crosses bridge and moves west; 850 Loyalists captured in the following days including weapons, wagon train, and £15,000 sterling; Loyalist military capacity in the Cape Fear region destroyed
Column in rout and dispersal; most captured within days; Brig. Gen. MacDonald surrenders April 1776; Highland Scots community loses its organized military arm
